summary: Iteration 2 validated: (1) complete shift removal from Global Filter Bar and API responses, (2) Dept -> Area -> Machine cascading, (3) new bulk runtime CSV import feature end-to-end. Backend: 21/21 pytest cases pass. Frontend: 27/28 UI checks pass (single failure is a cosmetic case-sensitivity mismatch in my selector, not a bug). All requested test IDs are present. Dry-run correctly detects ambiguity (PC21 without department) + unknown line code; commit correctly inserts and updates existing (line_id, date) pairs; template downloads with all expected columns.

minor.issue: Query param `shift` is silently accepted with 200 (ignored). Since Shift was intentionally removed, an explicit 422 for unknown query params would be cleaner, but 200-ignore is acceptable for backward compatibility.

integration_issues.flow: Bulk runtime import — second file upload without full re-select
integration_issues.issue: Chromium raised ERR_UPLOAD_FILE_CHANGED on second dry-run when the same file path was rewritten between uploads. UI showed a 'SOMETHING WENT WRONG' chip. Not a bug in the app; a Playwright-only artefact. Real users always pick a fresh File object. No fix required.

issues: Console still emits React hydration warning `<span> cannot be a child of <option>` on every render — same as iteration 1. Caused by editor tooling injecting instrumentation spans inside dynamic <option> children. Non-blocking but polluting the console.

action_items: OPTIONAL: address recurring React hydration warning around <option> children in filter selects (carried from iteration 1).
OPTIONAL: strip Mongo `_id` in services.notify() before hub.broadcast (carried from iteration 1).
OPTIONAL: derive real client IP from X-Forwarded-For for brute-force lockout (carried from iteration 1).
No blocking issues for the shift-removal / dept-cascade / bulk-runtime-import scope — feature is production-ready from a QA perspective.
critical_code_review_comments: runtime.py:114 — _parse_runtime_csv builds by_dept_code and by_code_only maps in-place; the AMBIGUOUS sentinel handling is elegant but rehearses the whole lines list on every request. For 2000+ lines this is fine, but consider caching the map for a few seconds if the endpoint becomes hot.

runtime.py:243 — dup_pairs formatting `f"{k[1]}/{k[0][:6]}"` truncates line_id to 6 chars — collides for lines whose UUIDs share a prefix. Consider using line_code (already resolved) for a stable human key.

runtime.py:255 — dry-run does one Mongo find_one per parsed row inside the loop (N+1). For a 1000-row CSV this is 1000 round-trips. Consider a single `find({line_id: {$in: [...]}, date: {$in: [...]}})` or a `$or` composite key aggregation, then in-memory match. Same issue in the commit handler.

runtime.py:249 — bulk_runtime_dry_run and bulk_runtime_commit both re-parse the CSV. For very large uploads the client must re-transmit the file twice. Consider a server-side staged buffer keyed by dry-run token so commit can replay the previously parsed rows.

runtime.py:180 — CSV parsing accepts empty strings for run/dark/calendar because _parse_float returns None on failure and the code correctly flags them, but a whitespace-only value would also silently parse to None; consider explicit strip+empty check.

analytics.py:127 — department_kpi has no `department` existence validation; requesting `/api/analytics/department/foo/kpi` returns 200 with zeros. Not a bug but a `400 UNKNOWN_DEPARTMENT` when department not in masters would be clearer.

GlobalFilterBar.jsx — the six <FilterCell> selects share identical structure; a small SelectCell helper would cut ~40 lines and avoid drift when adding a new dimension (esp. now that shift is being removed).

FilterContext.jsx line 145 — linesInScope memo does not scope by machine, which is fine; but machinesInScope filters `is_packing && kind !== 'stage'` — this business rule should be lifted to a shared helper (`isSelectableMachine`) so BreakdownsPage & AnalyticsPage don't diverge.
test_credentials: admin@factory.local / Admin@123 verified working via /api/auth/login (returns access_token in body + sets httpOnly cookies). Frontend stores access_token in localStorage key 'cmms.access_token' and sends as Bearer header. op@factory.local / Op@123 also verified for role-negative test (403 on /api/runtime/bulk-import/commit).
seed_data_creation: Backend suite writes TEST_-prefixed runtime rows for a uniquely-scoped line (first non-PC21 code) on 2026-03-01 and 2026-03-02, then updates them. UI suite writes PC21/process + PC21/packaging runtime rows on 2026-05-10 and 2026-05-20 (4 total). All rows are idempotent (upserted) — safe to re-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite is at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 21 tests, runs in ~2s under -n 2 --dist loadscope. Requires REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env var. Multipart uploads with a requests.Session that logged in via JSON MUST pass headers={'Content-Type': None} to let requests regenerate the multipart boundary — session-level Content-Type: application/json otherwise leaks through and causes 422 'field required'. For UI: frontend auth uses localStorage key 'cmms.access_token' (NOT httpOnly cookies from the browser's perspective — even though the server also sets cookies). After Playwright login+redirect, always page.reload() once to force FilterProvider to refetch masters with the newly-stored token, otherwise dept dropdown remains at 1 option. Both admin dropdowns render as native <select>, use page.select_option not click. PC21 exists in BOTH process and packaging (by design) — used as the ambiguity test case for bulk import.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration's scope (shift removal, dept cascading, bulk CSV import). All acceptance criteria met.
